title: Automation tiers enforce the configured approval path

steps.result: Successfully loaded Autopilot Browser Verification Project
Autopilot tab loaded with settings section
Dropdown expanded and all three options visible
Option selected successfully using agent-browser select command

browserVerification.finding: The automation tier dropdown is now interactable. Three tiers are available: Supervised (requires approval for each run), Semi-Auto (runs automatically, pauses on issues), Full Auto (fully autonomous). The dropdown options are visible and selectable. Previously this was a blocker. However, the approval enforcement behavior requires actual delivery run creation to fully validate - the UI shows the tier selection but not the downstream approval gate behavior.

dropdownOptions: Supervised — requires approval for each run
Semi-Auto — runs automatically, pauses on issues
evidence.networkCalls: GET delivery run endpoint was not directly tested via network inspection as no delivery runs section is visible in UI

title: Delivery run uses an isolated workspace and leased port

steps.result: No delivery runs section, runs list, or 'Create Delivery Run' button found
No workspace metadata section visible in the autopilot tab UI

browserVerification.finding: The backend registers data handlers for deliveryRuns, workspaceLeases, companyBudgets in worker.ts. However, the UI (src/autopilot/ui/index.tsx) does not have any component rendering delivery runs or workspace lease information. No delivery run creation form, runs list, or workspace metadata display exists in the autopilot tab surface. The UI sections found are: Autopilot Settings (with tier selector), Product Program Editor, Research, Ideas, Swipe Review, and Preference Profile. VAL-AUTOPILOT-032 cannot be validated in the browser without UI additions.

evidence.uiComponents: ResearchSection, IdeasSection, SwipeSection, PreferenceSection, AutopilotSettings, ProductProgramEditor - no DeliveryRunSection

steps.result: Budget tab loaded with '0.00' textbox and 'Update budget' button
Project-level budget field visible but company-wide budget cap display not visible

browserVerification.finding: The Budget tab shows a project-level budget input (textbox '0.00' with 'Update budget' button). There are also notifications with 'View run' and 'View agent' links suggesting some budget tracking exists. The backend has companyBudget data handlers that enforce pause behavior when autopilot budget minutes are exceeded (see worker.ts check: 'if (companyBudget.autopilotUsedMinutes >= companyBudget.autopilotBudgetMinutes) { companyBudget.paused = true; companyBudget.pauseReason = "Autopilot budget minutes exceeded"; }'). However, the UI does not display a 'paused' state for budgets in the Budget tab. The AutopilotSettings component shows a paused state for the autopilot itself (when autopilot.paused is true), but not for company budgets specifically. Budget cap enforcement cannot be fully tested in browser without setting actual budget values and exhausting them.

evidence.backendPauseLogic: check-budget-and-pause-if-needed action sets companyBudget.paused = true when autopilotUsedMinutes >= autopilotBudgetMinutes
evidence.consoleErrors: Multiple 502 Bad Gateway errors for failed resource loads

title: Operator can pause and resume autopilot or a specific run

steps.result: Autopilot settings loaded with automation tier selector
No Pause/Resume buttons found in autopilot settings or elsewhere in the tab

AutopilotSettings component has logic to display 'Paused:' message when autopilot.paused is true (line: 'autopilot && autopilot.paused && (
Paused: {autopilot.pauseReason}
)')

browserVerification.finding: The backend registers pauseAutopilot and resumeAutopilot actions (ACTION_KEYS.pauseAutopilot, ACTION_KEYS.resumeAutopilot in constants.ts, implemented in autopilot/worker.ts). The AutopilotSettings component has code to display a paused state message when autopilot.paused is true. However, no Pause/Resume buttons are visible in the UI - neither for the autopilot project level nor for individual delivery runs. The backend actions exist but no UI controls invoke them. Additionally, there is no delivery run section in the UI (see VAL-AUTOPILOT-032), so run-level pause/resume cannot be tested. The network calls for POST pause/resume were not exercised because no UI buttons exist to trigger them.

backendActions: pauseAutopilot - sets autopilot.paused = true with pauseReason
evidence.missingUI: No Pause/Resume buttons in autopilot settings section
